content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 25,
1992
Karen Knecht
Committee to Elect Karen Knecht
101 B.
W. McKnight Way,
No.
224
Grass Valley,
CA
95949
Re:
Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No.
1—92—478
Dear Ms.
Knecht:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the “Act”).1/
The Commission is not able to provide advice on
past conduct.
Therefore,
we are treating your letter as a request
for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c)(3).2/
QUESTION
You have asked how and when to report an outstanding bill on
your campaign statement when the bill is being disputed.
CONCLUSION
An outstanding debt must be reported on Schedule F,
Accrued
Expense Schedule,
during the period the goods or services were
received or,
in this case, during the period you received the
first invoice.
An explanation concerning the disputed billing
should be provided in the “Description of Outstanding Payment”
column.
1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et .
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114;
2 Cal.
Code of Regs.
Section
18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento. CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660


Karen Knecht
Our File No.
1—92—478
Page
2
FACTS
Your conuuittee received an invoice on March 1,
1992, for $225
from David DuPell of Public Resources of California for advice
provided to you by Mr. DuPell.
On September 14,
1992,
I spoke
with your campaign consultant,
Mr.
Daryl Shull,
who provided the
following additional information concerning the disputed bill.
At
the time Mr.
DuPell provided the campaign advice to you,
sometime
in February,
1992,
Mr.
DuPell was
a. consultant to the Friends of
Todd Juvinall committee and was contemplating working as a
consultant on your campaign.
Mr.
Shull indicated that at the time
Mr.
DuPell provided the advice he indicated that he would not bill
you for his time.
Approximately one month later you received an
invoice for $225 with instructions to make your payment payable to
the Friends of Todd Juvinall committee.
Your committee did not
pay the invoice because,
as stated in your letter, the Friends of
Todd Juvinall committee did not provide services to your
committee.
Your failure to pay the invoice resulted in a threat
of court action.
Subsequently,
Mr.
DuPell amended the invoice
requesting the payment be made out to David DuPell and not the
Friends of Todd Juvinall committee.
To avoid any court action,
your committee paid Mr.
DuPell $225.
ANALYSIS
Expenses you incur but do not pay during the period covered
by a campaign statement are “expenditures” and must be reported on
Schedule F.
The Act provides that an expenditure is made on the
date the payment is made or on the date consideration, if any, is
received, whichever is earlier.
(Section 82025.)
Section 84211
requires that all expenditures made during the period covered by
the campaign statement be disclosed on the campaign statement due
at the end of the reporting period.
From the facts presented, at the time Mr. DuPell’s services
were provided, it appears to have been your understanding that he
was providing voluntary personal services which would not
constitute a contribution to or a debt incurred by your campaign.
(Section 82015.)
However, your campaign was subsequently billed
for Mr. DuPell’s services.
Therefore, the committee should have


Karen Knecht
Our File No.
1—92—478
Page
3
reported the debt on the campaign statement covering the period in
which your campaign received the initial invoice from Mr.
DuPell.
If you have any questions concerning this matter, please
contact me at 916/322—5662.
Sincerely,
Mary
n Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

At the time Mr. DuPell provided the campaign advice to you, sometime in February, 1992, Mr. DuPell was a. consultant to the Friends of Todd Juvinall committee and was contemplating working as a consultant on your campaign. Mr. Shull indicated that at the time Mr. DuPell provided the advice he indicated that he would not bill you for his time.
